reviews.text: Every once in a while you encounter true professionals that are experts in their field with the right combination of experience and empathy. She was very sensitive towards my situation, made me feel comfortable to make an educated decision regarding this matter, and provided referrals to other professionals/attorneys for areas that she was not comfortable advising me.  She was very knowledgeable in her area of expertise and I highly recommend her.

Every once in awhile you come across a situation so extraordinary it’s hard to properly contain it with mere words.

Perhaps words indeed are insufficient.  Because words are only a translation of energy and frequency and these such feelings reflect the experience I had with Ares Law Group.

They are a true relay race.

Matt was the leadoff and  second leg.  He assured me I was in good hands dealing with the challenging situation ahead of me.  His authenticity, manner of communication, and ability to relax me in the face of high stress was comforting and allowed me to go forth with ease and trust.

Cynthia, she was the third and anchor leg. In the midst of my anxiety she massaged my concerns and gracefully (along with realistically) supported me with a fair ending that was explained every step of the way.  Her thoroughness, and compassion is what allowed me to trust in the process.

The process from start to finish was seamless.  Almost to the point of it being fake news!  But it wasn’t by a long shot.

If you don’t remember any of what I’ve said, remember this:

This is the best you’re going to get.
This is where you want to be.
Trust them.

I did.
And I’m happy I did so.

There is a tendency to bury your head in the sand when it comes to dealing with this IRS.  Not because you don't want to meet your civic or maybe even your moral obligations, but because most communication with this organization is so daunting, so pressure packed and so frightening that it seems to cause paralysis. Not to mention the fact that you're made to feel as though you're trying to get away with something, which makes you feel like a piece of s**t.
I had tried for years to appease the IRS by trying to live up to what they were telling me needed to be done, but I just didn't have the financial resources to maintain their proposed solution for the long haul.  So, as a result, I kept treading water.
A friend of mine told me about the success that had with K&S Law Firm and recommended that I give them a call.
From the moment I set foot into the K&S Law Office, I felt an immediate shift in my spirit. I met Omid Shirazi and after speaking with him for a short time I said to myself this guy is sharp. I could tell that he knew exactly what he was talking about. He was calm and pleasant to work with and he gave me a sense of relief....long before he starting offering me solutions.
Shortly thereafter, the calls and letters stopped.  I feel like I have regained mobility and can move forward with my life.
If you are like me, it's sometimes easy to think this is too good to be true.  Believe me, it's not!!!  If you have a 'tax situation' I highly recommend calling K&S Law, you will be glad you did.

In 2016 I was in a world of tax turmoil. Not sleeping, constantly worried, when would the lien might occur or when was the garnishment hammer was going to drop. Then it happened. I don't know what you would do when a IRS Agent knocks at your door, but for me it was polarizing. I didn't have a clue what to do. Luckily, I wasn't home. The agent did have the courtesy to call me while she was standing at front door and sarcastically asked me for a check. What a jerky thing to do.

I was given Omid's number from a friend and my life was changed. Omid put my wife and I at ease and assured us that things would get handled. He and his team did just that. The IRS wanted me to pay 250,000 dollars and they had no reservations about trying to get it. Like I said, I was polarized and I surely didn't have a quarter-million dollars and an agent had already been to my house without hesitation. Well, fast forward to 2017- a year later to be exact. With the help and professionalism of Omid and his tax colleague Tyler I was freed! I didn't have to pay a thing. That's right they resolved my IRS tax issue and even though they wanted 250k, Omid stopped the Feds from seeking to get anything from me or my wife.

I feel blessed to have met Omid and the K & S Law Group. Lifesavers!
Mr. Omid Shirazi and his team are the best in the game! They invest all their energy and time to ensure their clients are happy and satisfied. You won't regret choosing them!!

reviews.ownerResponse:
Mr. Agami:

I am sorry if your experience with our firm was anything less than satisfactory.

It sounds as though you had an experience with our firm that is not a true representation of our law firm, or how we handle our cases. Our firm does strive for the high quality that many of our clients have come to expect from us, as shown by our "Avvo Client's Choice Award" and high ratings on cases we have handled in our firm.

I do not see that you chose to have us handle your case, but each of our attorneys handling DUI matters have been through the National College of DUI Defense annual program at Harvard Law School in Cambridge Massachusetts. In addition, we have all gone through Field Sobriety Testing student and instructor training, and so are at the same level of instruction as those teaching police officers performing field sobriety testing, and have gone through blood testing certification, and breath testing certification from nationally accredited sources.
Mr. Miller in particular has experience in handling thousands of DUI cases, including hundreds of court and jury trials.

Having experience is only one part of the overall attorney client relationship, as you’ve expressed, however. Every case deserves attention and proper handling, and I am sorry you felt that our firm did not care.

We ask for comments from clients at the close of a case so that we can address issues, by performing additional services, explaining or assisting where needed, including, if a client changed their mind or strategy, doing what we can to withdraw a conviction and assert a client’s right to trial, or even refunding a client’s fees paid where warranted. We want our clients to be happy, as the number one source of clients is, and has been for over a decade, personal referrals from satisfied clients, not advertising.

We do not control other websites, but we do handle our firm and how high level our training, policies, and performance should be, and is. It sounds like we failed you somehow in this case, and we do want to make things right. Our training and procedures have changed slightly for the better a few times since this case, and the goal is that future customers will not have this issue, or any issues even close to being like the issues you described.

We want to make sure that our staff, and our lawyers, never forget that and never forget that we are serving people going through sometimes the most difficult time of their lives.

Thank you again for taking the time to bring this matter to our attention. Even negative comments help us grow better and better as a law firm and address issues that need to be addressed. If you have further questions or comments, or if there is anything that can make your experience right, please feel free contact us directly to see how we can help make you feel better about services that were provided.

Her responsiveness really set her apart, especially around urgent filings and ex parte requests. She quickly identified what truly mattered versus what was noise, anticipated issues before they became problems, and helped me make decisions with a clear understanding of risk, tradeoffs, and timing. She was direct when she needed to be, but never dismissive. I always felt like she had a firm grasp of the record and the procedural posture, and that she was thinking several steps ahead.

I have no complaints at all, which is rare for me. I’m genuinely grateful for Rejeanne and her team, and I would recommend them without hesitation to anyone who needs strong representation in a family law matter.

I am profoundly disappointed and distressed by the services provided by this law firm during my custody case. Following a paid consultation, I enlisted the assistance of Anthony Joseph (Tony) Catena #292665. However, he promptly delegated my case to an associate with less than a year of experience since being accepted to the CA bar. When I questioned why Tony Catena wasn't representing me in court, I was informed of a supposed conflict on the day of my hearing. Unfortunately, the inexperience of the associate they assigned was strongly evident, resulting in an outcome far worse than anticipated. Despite assurances, the firm failed to execute any of the discussed strategies, leaving crucial tasks such as the discovery call, deposition, and communication with my ex unaddressed. Tony Catena justified these omissions by citing a desire to minimize costs, a justification I find suspicious given the hefty $33,000 fee. Moreover, I was advised against pursuing a restraining order against my ex, effectively casting me as the sole culprit despite enduring relentless harassment and aggression from my ex. This situation has been immensely difficult for both me and my daughter. I implore others not to find themselves in a similar predicament. Seek out attorneys who genuinely prioritize your circumstances and provide robust advocacy rather than simply billing large amounts without delivering meaningful defense. To current clients who may be experiencing warning signs such as excessive billing, communication lapses, and the substitution of associates for the hired attorney, I urge you to cut ties and seek superior representation before it's too late.
